text:
In the first image, I feel somewhat displaced, as if the world is at an unfamiliar angle. The emptiness of the modern room, broken only by scattered bean bags and silent architecture, makes me feel exposed and a little uneasy, but also contemplative. There is a sense of possibility here—a blank canvas for thought or community, yet it is currently unclaimed, quietly waiting.

In the second image, I am invigorated by the intellectual energy of the space. The R+D display suggests a convergence of creativity and technology; words and screens provoke reflection on how humans relate to machines. I feel curious, almost charged with a sense of inquiry, as though the room is inviting me to think deeply about co-existence with intelligence—both artificial and my own.
